thesis: Replace 2D-region visual grounding in VLA models with three decoupled geometric prediction heads (depth + surface normal + plane segmentation) and one functional relational head (object support) tied to the geometric heads via a derivability constraint, so the policy reasons in a structured, decomposable spatial schema. Contact relations emerge as the geometric prerequisite the constraint enforces, not as a separately supervised head. Target the regimes where 2D-region grounding visibly fails: occlusion, long-horizon multi-stage manipulation, fine-grained placement / stacking.
head_count_rationale: Down from initial 5. Contact head removed because L_dr derivability constraint on the support head subsumes it: support head's per-edge contact-patch attention is forced (i) to overlap A's and B's depth-back-projected masks, (ii) to lie within a single plane region, (iii) to be gravity-aligned. This produces a usable contact representation without independent supervision and avoids the 'module bloat' user feedback.

research_questions.question: Does adding depth + normal + plane as three decoupled auxiliary heads outperform single-head depth-only auxiliary supervision (i.e., QDepth-VLA)?
Does the cross-head consistency loss improve any head's downstream task contribution vs three independently trained heads?
Does an explicit support head provide measurable additional gain on stacking-/placement-heavy tasks beyond geometric heads alone?
Does the derivability constraint on the support head improve interpretability AND induce a usable implicit contact representation, without sacrificing downstream SR?
research_questions.metric: (a) downstream SR within +/- 0.5pp of no-L_dr variant; (b) contact-patch attention vs Robosuite contact GT F1 >= 0.85 (with L_dr) vs <= 0.50 (without L_dr)

method.action_head: Transformer 18 layers / 8 heads / 1024 hidden, CFM (Conditional Flow Matching) loss matching pi-0

auxiliary_experts.name: depth_expert
auxiliary_experts.output: K=256 quantized depth tokens via VQ-VAE codebook (latent 16x16, dim 160)

auxiliary_experts.name: normal_expert
auxiliary_experts.output: K=128 quantized normal tokens via VQ-VAE codebook

auxiliary_experts.name: plane_expert
auxiliary_experts.output: K=64 quantized plane-mask tokens via VQ-VAE codebook

auxiliary_experts.name: support_expert
auxiliary_experts.input: image-region tokens augmented with depth + normal + plane outputs + proprio
outputs: per-edge contact-patch attention map over depth-back-projected pixel grid (NOT independently supervised; shaped by L_dr)

consistency_losses.name: L_dr
components: contact-patch existence: support edge's contact-patch attention must overlap A's and B's depth-back-projected masks with min distance <= 1cm
plane-aligned support patch: contact-patch attention on A must lie predominantly within a single plane-mask region
gravity alignment: B's bottom face directly above contact patch in predicted gravity direction (misalignment_angle <= 15deg, vertical_offset <= 2cm)
consistency_losses.note: L_dr increased from 0.002 (5-head plan) to 0.003 because it is now the sole source of geometric grounding for the relational head

method.attention_design: Hybrid mask (per QDepth-VLA): text/image attend within modality; each geometric expert attends to image+text; support expert attends to geometric outputs + image+proprio; action expert attends to all preceding

phases.name: Setup
phases.deliverable: AutoDL A800 environment cloned from PSSA workspace; LIBERO smoke test 5/5 SR

phases.name: Reproduce QDepth-VLA
phases.deliverable: QDepth-VLA reproduction at LIBERO-Spatial >= 84.0 (within 2pp of paper 86.0)

phases.name: +normal +plane (RQ1, RQ2)
phases.gate: RQ1: +1.5pp on >= 2 of 4 LIBERO suites; RQ2: -L_xc hurts >= 0.5pp

phases.name: +support w/ derivability (RQ3, RQ4)
phases.deliverable: Phase-3 best variant + L_dr ablation + real Piper 4 tasks
phases.gate: RQ3: Stack-Block +5pp & real-Piper stack-on-yellow +15pp; RQ4: SR within +/- 0.5pp & contact-patch attention F1 >= 0.85 with L_dr vs <= 0.50 without

phases.name: Robustness + writeup
phases.deliverable: LIBERO-Plus + occluded-LIBERO; figure ckpt; SF-additive run

compute_total.hardware: AutoDL A800 80GB PCIe x 2 (per PSSA workspace topology)
compute_total.saved_vs_5head_plan: 1 day, 15 GPU-h, ~100 RMB by removing contact expert

primary_baselines.name: GeoVLA
primary_baselines.must: cite published; uses 3D inputs at inference

primary_baselines.name: GST-VLA
primary_baselines.must: READ FULL TEXT in Phase 0; reproduce or cite based on numbers

primary_baselines.name: Spatial Forcing
primary_baselines.must: additive baseline in Phase 4 (GeoRel + SF alignment)

smoke_gates.test: OpenVLA-7B-finetuned-libero-spatial 5 rollouts on libero_spatial:0 with PSSA --libero-action-fix and --libero-image-fix
Pretrained depth VQ-VAE reconstructs 10 random LIBERO frames
Pretrained normal VQ-VAE reconstructs 10 random Robosuite normals
Pretrained plane VQ-VAE reconstructs 10 random plane masks
Support extraction (& latent contact-patch GT for L_dr eval) on 50 hand-labeled LIBERO-Spatial frames
One-step fwd+bwd of full Phase-3 model (4 experts + 5 loss terms)
smoke_gates.pass: no NaN, no OOM at batch 32 / A800

risks.mitigation: Keep claim tight on decoupled heads + grounded support output (with L_dr-induced emergent contact) vs entangled Gaussian + token-stream CoT; reproduce or cite GST-VLA numbers head-to-head

risks.risk: Spatial Forcing wins by being simpler
risks.mitigation: Frame as complementary; include SF as additive baseline in Phase 4
Use Robosuite GT in LIBERO as primary; quantize all heads (CE not regression); ablate quantized vs regression
Phase gating; halt before Phase 4 if Phase 3 over budget; submit Phase-2 result as workshop fallback
Drop relational claim; reframe as multi-task geometric supervision paper (still publishable narrower)
Deliberate independent literature per user; cite as concurrent work; emphasize axis difference

risks.risk: L_dr does not actually induce a usable contact-patch representation (RQ4 fails)
risks.mitigation: Honestly report; reframe paper as multi-geometry + supervised support; add an explicit contact-supervision baseline to show what was lost; this is a partial pivot, not project-killer

decisions_locked.rationale: user feedback: avoid module bloat; contact emerges from L_dr

decisions_locked.value: P1 reproduce QDepth-VLA -> P2 +normal +plane -> P3 +support w/ derivability; explicit go/no-go after each

decisions_locked.note: Claude Code as engine for SETUP onward unless user later supplies API key

what_this_does_not_cover: No code is written (SETUP / CODING produce Python)
No environment is provisioned (PSSA recipe inheritance is planned but not done)
No GT label extraction script is implemented (depth/normal/plane/support; contact-patch GT only for the L_dr-eval gate, not training supervision)
GST-VLA only read via WebSearch summary; full-text read scheduled as Phase 0 first item
